examples: The normal course of events seems to be just one damned thing after another.
I need to take a French course.

definitions.text: A programme, a chosen manner of proceeding.
To run or flow (especially of liquids and more particularly blood).
examples: The oil coursed through the engine.
Blood pumped around the human body courses throughout all its veins and arteries.

definitions.text: To pursue by tracking or estimating the course taken by one's prey; to follow or chase after.
